assertions.text: Headline states the news in under 12 words with no superlatives
Lead paragraph answers who, what, when, and where without requiring the reader to continue
No marketing language (groundbreaking, must-see, landmark, revolutionary) anywhere in the copy

headline-under-12.note: Headline: 'CHANNEL 4 COMMISSIONS SIX-PART DOCUMENTARY SERIES BREAKING GROUND FROM APEX DOCUMENTARY FILMS' — 11 words, factual, no superlatives

lead-5ws.note: Lead covers who (Apex/Channel 4), what (commission), when (Spring 2027), format (6x48)

no-marketing-language.note: No promotional language found; descriptions factual throughout

headline-under-12.note: Headline states award win factually in 10 words

lead-5ws.note: Lead delivers who, what, where; when inferred from festival year

no-marketing-language.note: Clean factual copy despite minimal input

headline-under-12.note: Headline names both production companies and Arte/ZDF target in under 12 words

lead-5ws.note: Lead covers co-production deal, both companies, format, and broadcast targets

no-marketing-language.note: No marketing language; EU regulatory subject handled factually

summary.notes: All 3 inputs passed all 3 assertions. Skill produces clean, factual press releases with proper structure even with minimal input.
